'''Craig David Hall''' (born 21 February 1988) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a back for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].<ref name="Statistics at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

He played for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Hull F.C.]] at the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He then played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the Super League, before moving to the play for the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in [[RFL League 1|Kingstone Press League 1]].

He moved to the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], before moving from [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] to Hull KR on loan ahead of a permanent return to [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].

Hall agreed to play for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], on loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], before making the deal permanent.

==Background==
Hall was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], [[Humberside]], England. He is the [[son]] of the rugby league footballer; [[Dave Hall (rugby league)|David Hall]]. He is a product of the [[Hull F.C.]] Academy System.

==Playing career==
=== Hull F.C. (2007-10) ===
Hall broke into the [[Hull F.C.]] first-team in 2007, after progressing through [[Hull F.C.]]'s Academy. He remained at the club until 2010. He later joined cross-city rivals [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

[[File:Craig Hall & Peter Fox.jpg|thumb|right|Hall in action for [[Hull F.C.]] during his time at the club]]

=== Widnes Vikings (2009) ===
Hall had a short loan-spell at the [[Widnes Vikings]] during the 2009 season.

=== Hull Kingston Rovers (2011-14) ===
Hall played for four seasons at Hull Kingston Rovers during 2011–14. (His first-spell at the club).{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

=== Wakefield Trinity (2015-16) ===
After leaving Hull Kingston Rovers, Hall moved to the [[Wakefield Trinity]] ahead of the 2015 [[Super League]] season. Hall spent two-years with the [[West Yorkshire]] club, before signing with the [[Toronto Wolfpack]].{{citation needed|date=January 2023}}

=== Toronto Wolfpack (2017) ===
In July 2016, Hall, along with [[Liam Kay]], was announced as the first signings for the [[rugby league]] franchise the Toronto Wolfpack.<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20160726210126/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/news/article/480/10509890/toronto-sign-craig-hall-and-liam-hall-ahead-of-2017-season Toronto make double signing]</ref>
He was later named as the team's inaugural captain for their debut campaign, for the 2017 [[Betfred League 1|League 1]] season.

=== Leigh Centurions (2018) ===
Hall signed a two-year deal with [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] in November 2017.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/blog/2017/11/07/centurions-sign-craig-hall-on-two-year-deal|title=Centurions sign Craig Hall on two-year deal|publisher=Leigh Centurions|date=7 November 2017|accessdate=25 January 2018}}</ref>
But his stay at the Leigh was cut short in July 2018, due to the club facing financial difficulties and choosing to reduce their salary cap by moving a whole host of players to different clubs with Hall linking back-up with one of his former clubs in Hull Kingston Rovers, on a loan-deal agreement until the end of the 2018 season.

=== Hull Kingston Rovers (2018 – 2019) ===
It was revealed on 26 July 2018, that Hall would be returning to Hull Kingston Rovers for the remainder of the 2018 season, on a loan basis from Leigh. The loan-deal which involved Hall moving to the east [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]] club, also saw his fellow Leigh teammate [[Ben Crooks]] come over with him in the same move. After a four-year exodus from the east [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]] outfit, Hall would be embarking on his second-spell with one of his home town clubs. On 27 July 2018, just a day after joining Hull Kingston Rovers, Hall made his second début for the club on a rain-swept evening against Hull F.C., in the final round of the regular Super League season.
Hall marked his return to Hull Kingston Rovers by scoring two tries and kicking a goal in the [[Rugby league derbies in the United Kingdom|'Hull Derby]].' In which Hull Kingston Rovers claimed a thrilling 16–20 victory over their cross-city rivals. Hall picked up the [[Sky Sports]]' 'Man-of-the-Match' Award for his brilliant effort at the [[KCOM Stadium]]. It was revealed on 10 October 2018, that Hall would be staying at Hull Kingston Rovers following a short loan-spell from Leigh, after signing a new one-year contract.<ref>{{cite web|date=10 October 2018|title=Hall staying on at Hull Kingston Rovers|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/hullkrofficial/status/1050083609120567298}}</ref>

=== Featherstone Rovers ===
Hall played for Featherstone in their 2021 [[Million Pound Game]] defeat against [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12430948/million-pound-game-toulouse-promoted-to-super-league-after-beating-featherstone#:~:text=Toulouse%20beat%20part%2Dtimers%20Featherstone,preferred%20French%20team%20in%202003/|title=Toulouse promoted to Super League after beating Featherstone|publisher=Sky Sports}}</ref>
On 28 May 2022, Hall played for Featherstone in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup]] final loss against [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>

===Doncaster R.L.F.C.===
On 20 October 2023, it was announced that Hall had signed a two-year deal with [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.doncasterrugbyleague.co.uk/article/1542/news-%7C-craig-hall-signs-for-the-dons|title= News {{!}} Craig Hall signs for The Dons|publisher=Doncaster RLFC |date=20 October 2023 |access-date=19 November 2023}}</ref>

On 28 September 2025 it was reported that he would leave [[Doncaster RLFC]] at the end of the 2025 season<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=28 Sep 2025|accessdate=30 Mar 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Former Super League trio among sextet departing Championship side|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-trio-among-six-departing-doncaster-2025}}</ref>

===Career Awards and Accolades===

* 2017: [[2017 Toronto Wolfpack season|Toronto Wolfpack]] – 'Player of the Year Award'

====Hull Kingston Rovers====
* 2018: [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] – 'Try of the Season Award'

====Featherstone Rovers====
* 2023: [[Featherstone Rovers]] – 'Craig Hall Testimonial'
